tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.usub.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32) #0

define i32 @test5(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @test5(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[COND:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[A:%.*]], [[B:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[COND]], label [[BB1:%.*]], label [[BB3:%.*]]
; CHECK:       bb1:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 false, label [[BB3]], label [[BB2:%.*]]
; CHECK:       bb2:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
; CHECK:       bb3:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
  %cond = icmp eq i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb3

bb1:                                              ; preds = %0
  %sub1 = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.usub.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %r1 = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %sub1, 0
  %c1 = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %sub1, 1
  br i1 %c1, label %bb3, label %bb2

bb2:                                              ; preds = %bb1
  ret i32 %r1

bb3:                                              ; preds = %bb1, %0
  ret i32 0
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb3

bb1:                                              ; preds = %0
  %sub1 = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.usub.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %r1 = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %sub1, 0
  %c1 = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %sub1, 1

bb2:                                              ; preds = %bb1

define i32 @srem_domcondition(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @srem_domcondition(
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[CMP:%.*]] = icmp eq i32 [[X:%.*]], [[Y:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[CMP]], label [[COND_TRUE:%.*]], label [[COND_END:%.*]]
; CHECK:       cond.true:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[COND_END]]
; CHECK:       cond.end:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 0
entry:
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %x, %y
  br i1 %cmp, label %cond.true, label %cond.end

cond.true:                                        ; preds = %entry
  %srem = srem i32 %x, %y
  br label %cond.end

cond.end:                                         ; preds = %cond.true, %entry
  %cond = phi i32 [ %srem, %cond.true ], [ 0, %entry ]
  ret i32 %cond
}

issue.title: Conditional constant propagation suboptimal case
issue.author: bcl5980

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
This type of transform usually happens in:
llvm::simplifyWithOpReplaced()
...so stepping through that code might show why we missed the transform for this example.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: I believe this is because we only replace values one level up, while this requires looking back two instructions.

comments.author: bcl5980
comments.body:
Maybe we can call isImpliedByDomCondition in computeKnownBitsAddSub to set KnownBits for this case.
But I'm a little worry about the CPU overhead. And I don't know it is a good way or not.
